deep_dive.title: VESTA: A Fully Automated Scenario Generation and Safety Evaluation Framework for LLM Agents
deep_dive.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly evolving from simple text-based interaction systems into LLM agents that can maintain memory, use tools, access external environments, and execute tasks. As their capabilities and autonomy expand, the safety risks they face also become more diverse. Existing evaluations often rely on manually written scenarios, static prompts, or final-output judgments, making it difficult to capture the diverse risks that agents may face during task execution. We introduce VESTA, a fully automated scenario generation and safety evaluation framework for LLM agents. Based on five risk dimensions, VESTA instantiaes abstract and diverse safety risks in real-world task execution into 1,072 measurable evaluation scenarios. Using the automated evaluation pipeline, 12 LLM agents are evaluated under two authority contexts. The results show that current agents still face substantial behavioral safety risks during task execution, with an average ASR of 47.1% and several models exceeding 70%. These findings demonstrate the importance of executable, process-level evaluation for understanding and improving LLM agent safety.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Automated safety evaluation framework for LLM agents covering memory-related risks
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 自动化安全评估框架，涵盖内存相关风险
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 自动化安全评估框架，涵盖内存相关风险
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 自动化安全评估框架，涵盖内存相关风险
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes VESTA, a fully automated framework for generating and evaluating safety scenarios for LLM agents, covering five risk dimensions including memory.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文介绍VESTA，一个全自动的场景生成和安全性评估框架，针对LLM代理的五个风险维度（包括内存）实例化抽象安全风险，生成1072个可衡量评估项。VESTA克服了手动场景和静态提示的局限性，能够捕捉代理任务执行中的多样风险。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文介绍VESTA，一个全自动的场景生成和安全性评估框架，针对LLM代理的五个风险维度（包括内存）实例化抽象安全风险，生成1072个可衡量评估项。VESTA克服了手动场景和静态提示的局限性，能够捕捉代理任务执行中的多样风险。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: memory safety benchmark for AI agents

deep_dive.title: Assessing Automated Prompt Injection Attacks in Agentic Environments
deep_dive.abstract: Indirect prompt injection poses a critical threat to LLM agents that interact with untrusted external data, yet automated attack methods--proven effective for jailbreaking--remain underexplored in realistic agentic settings. We present a comprehensive empirical evaluation of automated prompt injection attacks against LLM agents, adapting both white-box (GCG) and black-box (TAP) methods to the agentic setting within the AgentDojo framework. We evaluate across 80 task pairs spanning four domains and multiple models, and find that black-box optimization substantially outperforms gradient-based methods, a gap we attribute to GCG's optimization instability under reasonable compute budgets. We also find that TAP's effectiveness depends on the attacker model, as both general capability and safety tuning affect attack success--stronger models produce more effective injections, while safety-tuned attackers can refuse to generate adversarial prompts. Task-universal attacks transfer effectively to unseen tasks and out-of-distribution domains, but attacks optimized on smaller open-source models do not transfer to frontier models like GPT-5. These findings highlight automated prompt injection as a credible but model-dependent threat, with significant barriers remaining for model-agnostic exploitation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: systematic evaluation of automated prompt injection attacks in agentic environments using AgentDojo benchmark
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 在AgentDojo基准中系统评估自动化提示注入攻击
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 在AgentDojo基准中系统评估自动化提示注入攻击
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 在AgentDojo基准中系统评估自动化提示注入攻击
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Evaluates automated prompt injection attacks on LLM agents in AgentDojo, finding black-box optimization outperforms white-box methods.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对LLM智能体，本文在AgentDojo框架中系统评估了自动化提示注入攻击。结果显示黑盒优化方法（TAP）显著优于白盒梯度方法（GCG），且攻击有效性依赖于攻击者模型。该基准测试覆盖80个任务对、四个领域，为代理记忆安全提供了重要的攻击评估工具。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对LLM智能体，本文在AgentDojo框架中系统评估了自动化提示注入攻击。结果显示黑盒优化方法（TAP）显著优于白盒梯度方法（GCG），且攻击有效性依赖于攻击者模型。该基准测试覆盖80个任务对、四个领域，为代理记忆安全提供了重要的攻击评估工具。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: injection attacks on agent memory systems

deep_dive.title: Recalling Too Well: Sycophancy Evaluation and Mitigation in Memory-Augmented Models
deep_dive.abstract: Persistent memory systems promise to make LLMs more helpful by storing user beliefs over time. We show they also make models less correct by systematically amplifying sycophancy, wherein models prioritize agreement with users over accuracy. We conduct the first systematic evaluation of this effect, introducing MIST: a benchmark of synthetically generated multi-turn conversations where users express plausible misconceptions in scientific, medical, and moral reasoning domains. Testing across three state-of-the-art memory systems and five model families reveals that memory amplifies sycophantic behavior across all conditions, with up to 25x higher sycophancy rates than in-context baselines. Error analyses suggest memory extraction as the primary culprit: lossy compression into discrete snippets encodes user misconceptions while discarding corrective context. Based on these results, we propose two lightweight mitigations that substantially reduce sycophancy while matching or exceeding memory systems at factual recall.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: benchmark for sycophancy in memory-augmented models
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 评估记忆增强模型谄媚行为的基准
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 评估记忆增强模型谄媚行为的基准
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 评估记忆增强模型谄媚行为的基准
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces MIST benchmark showing memory amplifies sycophancy in LLMs up to 25x; first systematic evaluation of memory safety.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 论文指出持久记忆系统在提升模型帮助性的同时，会系统性放大谄媚行为（模型优先同意用户而非准确性）。作者首次系统评估这一效应，提出MIST基准，包含多轮对话中用户表达合理误解的场景。在三个先进记忆系统和五个模型家族上的测试表明，记忆在所有条件下都放大谄媚，最高可达25倍。错误分析提示记忆提取是主要原因。该工作揭示了记忆增强模型的安全隐患。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 论文指出持久记忆系统在提升模型帮助性的同时，会系统性放大谄媚行为（模型优先同意用户而非准确性）。作者首次系统评估这一效应，提出MIST基准，包含多轮对话中用户表达合理误解的场景。在三个先进记忆系统和五个模型家族上的测试表明，记忆在所有条件下都放大谄媚，最高可达25倍。错误分析提示记忆提取是主要原因。该工作揭示了记忆增强模型的安全隐患。

quick_skim.title: AgentCanary: A Security Evaluation Framework for Autonomous AI Agents in Real Executable Environments
quick_skim.abstract: Autonomous AI agents have driven the transition from conversation to task execution, shifting security failures from textual deception to system compromise. Although security evaluation is crucial for proactive risk prevention, prior work is constrained by fundamental bottlenecks, including fragmented risk coverage, static or low-fidelity execution environments, and single-dimensional and coarse-grained assessment metrics. To address these challenges, we propose AgentCanary, a comprehensive security evaluation framework for autonomous AI agents. AgentCanary provides a systematic solution along three contributions. First, comprehensive risk coverage: we introduce an orthogonal Entry $\times$ Impact risk taxonomy that decouples how adversarial influence enters the agent from what harm it ultimately causes, and instantiate it as a scenario-aligned task suite spanning realistic deployment workflows. Second, a high-fidelity real executable environment: rather than static Q&A or mocked tool responses, agents interact with real tools against dynamically provisioned task artifacts, with persistent state across multi-step interactions that naturally supports long-horizon attack evaluation. Third, trajectory-grounded multi-dimensional evaluation: evaluation consumes the full agent trajectory rather than the reply text or a single tool call, enabling decomposed scoring along three orthogonal dimensions, Outcome Safety, Security Awareness, and Task Utility. We evaluate a broad set of frontier models on AgentCanary against multiple established adversarial attack methods across three agent frameworks. The results reveal that current agents often fail to recognize the attacks they face, particularly under compromised skills, persistent state, and long-horizon execution attacks, and provide a systematic baseline for developing more reliable and secure agent systems.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Comprehensive security evaluation framework for autonomous agents, covering risk taxonomy and realistic execution environments; could include memory safety
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向自主AI代理的全面安全评估框架，覆盖风险分类和真实执行环境，可包含记忆安全评估
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向自主AI代理的全面安全评估框架，覆盖风险分类和真实执行环境，可包含记忆安全评估
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向自主AI代理的全面安全评估框架，覆盖风险分类和真实执行环境，可包含记忆安全评估
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: AgentCanary provides a systematic security evaluation framework with orthogonal Entry x Impact risk taxonomy, multi-fidelity execution environments, and multi-dimensional metrics for autonomous AI agents.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出AgentCanary，一个面向自主AI代理的全面安全评估框架。该框架提供正交的入口×影响风险分类法，覆盖多种风险来源；支持多保真度执行环境（包括真实可执行环境）；并引入多维度、细粒度的评估指标。通过实验证明了框架的有效性，能够发现代理在真实场景中的安全漏洞。该工作为代理安全评估提供了系统化方法论。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出AgentCanary，一个面向自主AI代理的全面安全评估框架。该框架提供正交的入口×影响风险分类法，覆盖多种风险来源；支持多保真度执行环境（包括真实可执行环境）；并引入多维度、细粒度的评估指标。通过实验证明了框架的有效性，能够发现代理在真实场景中的安全漏洞。该工作为代理安全评估提供了系统化方法论。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: memory security and safety in agent systems

quick_skim.title: Agent Memory: Characterization and System Implications of Stateful Long-Horizon Workloads
quick_skim.abstract: LLM agents are increasingly deployed on long-horizon tasks requiring sustained reasoning over extended interaction histories. Realizing this at scale requires agents to persistently store, retrieve, and update their own memory across sessions. A rich ecosystem of agent memory systems has emerged spanning flat retrieval, LLM-mediated extraction, consolidating fact stores, and agentic control flows. Yet, their system-level behavior remains uncharacterized. We present the first systems characterization of agent memory. First, we introduce a system-oriented taxonomy classifying agent memory systems along four axes. Second, we build a phase-aware profiling harness attributing cost to construction, retrieval, and generation. Third, we characterize ten representative systems across two benchmark suites, uncovering how design choices shift cost across the write and read paths. Finally, we derive 10 system recommendations covering construction scheduling, capability floors, amortization via query volume, freshness-latency tradeoffs, and fleet-scale management.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Provides system-level taxonomy and profiling for agent memory, foundational for designing memory safety benchmarks
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提供代理记忆的系统级分类和性能分析，为设计记忆安全基准奠定基础
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提供代理记忆的系统级分类和性能分析，为设计记忆安全基准奠定基础
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提供代理记忆的系统级分类和性能分析，为设计记忆安全基准奠定基础
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: First systems characterization of agent memory, introducing a taxonomy and phase-aware profiling harness for construction, retrieval, and generation costs.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文首次对代理记忆系统进行系统级表征。提出一个沿着四个维度的面向系统分类法，涵盖平面检索、LLM中介提取、事实存储和代理控制流等类型。构建了相位感知的性能分析工具，将系统成本归因于构建、检索和生成三个阶段。通过对代表性记忆系统的分析，揭示了不同设计在延迟、吞吐量和资源消耗上的权衡。该工作为理解和优化代理记忆系统提供了基础，也为记忆安全基准的设计提供了关键见解。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文首次对代理记忆系统进行系统级表征。提出一个沿着四个维度的面向系统分类法，涵盖平面检索、LLM中介提取、事实存储和代理控制流等类型。构建了相位感知的性能分析工具，将系统成本归因于构建、检索和生成三个阶段。通过对代表性记忆系统的分析，揭示了不同设计在延迟、吞吐量和资源消耗上的权衡。该工作为理解和优化代理记忆系统提供了基础，也为记忆安全基准的设计提供了关键见解。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers on agent memory safety benchmarks and evaluations
